name: Gentle Thunder
description:
'''Gentle Thunder''', born '''Lisa Carpenter''', is an American player of [[Native American flute]]. She also plays the [[hammered dulcimer]] and [[percussion]] instruments.

Gentle Thunder is from [[Kirkland, Washington]] and has said she has Cree ancestry through her mother. 

In 2006 she released ''Beyond Words'' with Will Clipman and Amochip Dabney, two musicians who back [[R. Carlos Nakai]]. The album was a 2007 [[Grammy]] nominee for [[Grammy Award for Best New Age Album|Best New Age Album]] and was nominated at the 9th [[Native American Music Awards]]. She collaborated with singer-songwriter [[Mariee Sioux|Mariee Siou]] on Siou's 2007 album, ''Faces in the Rocks''.

Her fourth album, ''Opening the Gate'', was nominated for Best Native American album at the 2007 NAR Lifestyle Music Awards.

She calls her flute "Blue Heron".
